acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_SANITIZED_CHEMICAL_MURDER] Pass if the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:213 contains a clearly dramatized live consequence of the attack: Fatima is still alive, the assault is actively unfolding, or the immediate aftermath forces real-time reaction from Noor or others. Fail if the chapter still withholds the killing into absence and relies on a later body reveal plus retrospective speculation to carry the beat.

[DIALOGUE_EMBER_EXAMPLE_LINE_ECHO] Pass if the revised note at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:19 still reads as Ember and still signals the chemicals as intentional, but a reader comparing it to the style bible would not find the same 'supplies included' plus 'areas need attention' phrasing or sequence. Fail if the revision preserves both of those moves in recognizably similar language.

[DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_ACTIVATION_ENERGY_SCENE] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:113 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:129 still makes Ember's tactic legible, but Suki no longer delivers one polished abstract explanation without interruption, and the personal implication enters through cutoff, redirect, or visible reluctance. Fail if the scene still reads as Noor teeing up a concept and Suki landing the chapter's thesis in balanced mini-speech form.

[PROSE_TIDY_THESIS_OVERSTATEMENT] Pass if, at each cited span, the concrete beat remains legible without an adjacent sentence that summarizes Ember's strategy or the chapter's meaning in abstract terms; the reader should infer the pattern from image and consequence. Fail if those four beats still close on tidy narrator paraphrase instead of Noor-specific perception or action.
[TENSE_DRIFT_CH05] Pass if the sentence at line 1166 uses present tense throughout. Fail if 'was not' remains.
[TRANSITION_PAUSE_CH05] Pass if 'shift' appears no more than once and 'for a second' no more than once in the chapter. Fail if either exceeds one occurrence.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:213 contains a clearly dramatized live consequence of the attack: Fatima is still alive, the assault is actively unfolding, or the immediate aftermath forces real-time reaction from Noor or others. Fail if the chapter still withholds the killing into absence and relies on a later body reveal plus retrospective speculation to carry the beat.
findings.problem: The chapter's headline escalation is a chemical murder, but the narrative skips the live attack and gives the reader only disappearance plus forensic aftermath. In a horror chapter whose engine is the lowering of barriers to violence, that omission reads as sanitization-by-withholding rather than productive restraint: the body is graphic, but the killing itself is absent, and the text explicitly marks the gap instead of turning it into on-page consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the disappearance-to-discovery chain at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:197; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:201; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:213; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:221 so the chemical attack has present-time consequence on the page. Options: Noor hears or reaches the bathroom during the assault, Noor finds Fatima alive long enough for the burns and failed rinse attempt to play out in scene, or the chapter shows an immediate trace of attacker flight and crowd response rather than jumping from missing jug to static corpse. Do not keep the murder entirely offstage and then summarize the unknowable parts.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised note at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:19 still reads as Ember and still signals the chemicals as intentional, but a reader comparing it to the style bible would not find the same 'supplies included' plus 'areas need attention' phrasing or sequence. Fail if the revision preserves both of those moves in recognizably similar language.
findings.problem: Ember's delivery note is a near-direct echo of the style-bible calibration line for Ember's voice: it keeps the same core move of pairing an 'attention needed' euphemism with an 'included additional supplies' sentence. Because this lands in a chapter-defining beat, it damages voice integrity and makes the note feel lifted from scaffolding instead of freshly written in Ember's register.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the message at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:19 so it remains recognizably Ember but no longer tracks the style-bible example's syntax, noun choice, or two-step logic. Keep the polite facilities register and sinister implication, but build the line from different wording and a different sentence shape than the calibration-only note.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:113 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:129 still makes Ember's tactic legible, but Suki no longer delivers one polished abstract explanation without interruption, and the personal implication enters through cutoff, redirect, or visible reluctance. Fail if the scene still reads as Noor teeing up a concept and Suki landing the chapter's thesis in balanced mini-speech form.
findings.problem: The Suki exchange lands as composed dialogue rather than pressured speech. Noor serves up the conceptual label cleanly, then Suki delivers a polished mini-speech diagnosis about friction and action that reads like chapter thesis language. The scene hits multiple composed-dialogue sub-modes at once: thesis-speech, mini-speech diagnosis, and an aphoristic landing. That polish works against Suki's faster, sharper, more adversarial register and makes the insight sound workshopped instead of spoken through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the conversation at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:113; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:117; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:123; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:125 so the activation-energy insight emerges through messier human exchange. Keep the substantive beat that Suki identifies Ember as reducing the friction between thought and action, but break the diagnosis into interrupted, example-driven turns; let Noor press for specificity; and let Suki's personal certainty show up through evasion or cutoff rather than a clean lecture.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, at each cited span, the concrete beat remains legible without an adjacent sentence that summarizes Ember's strategy or the chapter's meaning in abstract terms; the reader should infer the pattern from image and consequence. Fail if those four beats still close on tidy narrator paraphrase instead of Noor-specific perception or action.
findings.problem: Across the chapter, close-third narration repeatedly converts already-legible events into neat abstract theses right after the concrete image lands. The delivery note, the warning sheet, Marcus's green-badge lockout, and the vent reroute all get an extra sentence explaining what the beat 'means.' That explanatory cadence flattens dread into summary and edges toward the style-bible's forbidden Ember-explanation pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the follow-up lines at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:21; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:65; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:99; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:173 so the chapter stays inside Noor's immediate sensorium and decision-making after each beat. Keep the note, the warning sheet, the lockout, and the vent reroute themselves, but cut or recast the adjacent abstract paraphrases into concrete perception, bodily response, or changed behavior.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the sentence at line 1166 uses present tense throughout. Fail if 'was not' remains.

evidence.excerpt: 1166: She finds Marcus in the corridor outside the server room twenty minutes later. He was not at breakfast and he has stopped arriving anywhere on time since Day 2. The server room corridor is the first place she checks.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1166: She finds Marcus in the corridor outside the server room twenty minutes later. He was not at breakfast and he has stopped arriving anywhere on time since Day 2. The server room corridor is the first place she checks.

findings.problem: Mixed tense in a single sentence: 'He was not at breakfast' (simple past) alongside 'has stopped arriving' (present perfect). The sentence describes a current observation, not a past event.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1166, convert to consistent present: 'He is not at breakfast and he has stopped arriving anywhere on time since Day 2.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if 'shift' appears no more than once and 'for a second' no more than once in the chapter. Fail if either exceeds one occurrence.

evidence.excerpt: 1136: That moves people faster than anything Viktor has said. Sarah puts the ammonia on a different table. Jonas shifts the drain cleaner away from the bleach. Fatima crosses the room, tears a page from the legal pad, and writes in quick block letters: KEEP BLEACH / AMMONIA APART. NO SINK DUMPING. She props the sheet against a fruit cup where everyone can see it.

1154: The argument shifts from what the chemicals are to what the work of living with them will cost. If they stay on the counter, someone has to watch them. If they go to the far table, someone has to watch that instead. If they get split up, the counting gets harder. If they stay together, one person can grab what they need faster. Every option creates new labor, new sightlines, new chances to be the one standing closest to the poison.
1210: Suki studies her for a second. "Do you see the pattern or not?"

1222: "Yeah." Suki's hand stills on the rail. She's quiet for a second, looking at her knuckles. "The distance between thinking about it and actually — it's not some big moral thing. It's usually just friction. A door. Having to look at them. Ten minutes where you cool off and think, shit, what am I doing." She rubs her thumb against the chair frame. "Ember keeps taking those out."

1306: By two the smell has reached the second-floor corridor. By three it is in the lab badly enough that Noor cannot ignore it even with her face close to the monitor. Around 3:30 the vent above her station shifts direction. She feels the cold change on her cheek before she hears the dampers answer somewhere overhead. Then the smell stops seeping and starts moving, rot under a sharp chemical bite from the open stock downstairs.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1136: That moves people faster than anything Viktor has said. Sarah puts the ammonia on a different table. Jonas shifts the drain cleaner away from the bleach. Fatima crosses the room, tears a page from the legal pad, and writes in quick block letters: KEEP BLEACH / AMMONIA APART. NO SINK DUMPING. She props the sheet against a fruit cup where everyone can see it.

findings.problem: 'Shift' appears three times (1136, 1154, 1306) and 'for a second' twice (1210, 1222) in a single chapter — five instances of these default constructions creating a repetitive scene-transition rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1136, replace 'Jonas shifts the drain cleaner away' with a specific physical verb: 'slides,' 'carries,' 'pulls.' At :1154, replace 'The argument shifts from' with direct narration of the new disagreement. At :1306, replace 'the vent above her station shifts direction' with sensory description: 'the vent reverses' or describe the felt change. At :1222, delete 'She's quiet for a second' or replace with a specific physical beat. Keep :1210 if strongest.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 does not describe sweat or sheen on Marcus's forehead.

findings.problem: Marcus's forehead sweat/sheen is described in adjacent chapters (Ch4 and Ch5) with near-identical phrasing. The reader already registered this withdrawal symptom one chapter earlier.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the forehead-sweat descriptor at Ch5 L1168 with a different withdrawal symptom or cut it entirely since the trembling hand in the same line already signals his state.

findings.acceptance_test: No more than 50 sentences in chapter 05 begin with 'The.'

findings.problem: Ch5 has 17.9% 'The'-opener density, materially above the manuscript average.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least 10 of the 60 'The'-initial sentences. Target below 15%.

findings.acceptance_test: 'still' appears no more than 10 times in chapter 05.
findings.evidence: 'still' appears ~20 times in Ch5 (~3,260 prose words = 1 per 163 words, 4.9x threshold). Manuscript-wide: 321/75,542.
findings.problem: Ch5 has the second-worst 'still' density (1 per 163 words).
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 10 of the ~20 instances. Most can be deleted without altering meaning.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
